task.title: Setting language-specific preferences for comparing files

titlealts.navtitle: Setting language-specific preferences for comparing files
titlealts.searchtitle: Compare/Patch preferences for COBOL, PL/I, and JCL files

task.shortdesc: You can set language-specific preferences for comparing COBOL, PL/I, and JCL files from the menu bar, in the compare editor, or in the apply patch window.

prereq.p:
This topic explains how to set preferences for the Text Compare editor. For information about how to set preferences for the LPEX compare tool, see
Preferences - Compare.

taskbody.context:
Typically, the sequence number area is in the following locations:
COBOL files: Columns 1-6.
PL/I files: Columns 73-80
JCL files: Columns 73-80 In COBOL files, the information area is typically the eight columns to the right of margin R, for example, columns 73-80. For AIX COBOL, the information area can span columns 73-80 or 253-260 depending on the SRCFORMAT option setting. For MVS COBOL, the information area is columns 73-80.

substep.cmd: On the Preferences window, do one of these steps:
To ignore the sequence area when you compare files, select Ignore sequence area.

substep.note: To ignore the information area when you compare files, select Ignore information area.
To ignore case, select Ignore case.

substep.cmd: To save the settings, click OK.

step.cmd: On the toolbar or menu of the compare editor or on the menu of the apply patch window:

substep.cmd: To ignore the COBOL information area or the JCL and PL/I sequence area, click Ignore information area or press Alt+Shift+].

substep.note: To ignore the COBOL sequence area, click Ignore sequence number area or press Alt+Shift+[.

link.linktext: COBOL AIX Programming Reference (search for SRCFORMAT compiler option)
